# Phase 10.0.16 Wisdom Prudence Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated wisdom-authority systems, centralized judgment-enforcement systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve institutional wisdom, prudence, long-horizon judgment, restraint under pressure, disciplined decision-making, and strategic humility after peaceful transition and succession stability doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional wisdom-preservation doctrine.
- Institutional prudence survivability philosophy.
- Long-horizon judgment continuity architecture.
- Governance restraint under pressure doctrine.
- Civilization disciplined-decision framework.
- Constitutional prudence continuity standards.
- Anti-short-termism governance philosophy.
- Institutional wisdom-transfer survivability doctrine.
- Governance patience and restraint architecture.
- Civilization strategic humility framework.
- Phase 10.0.16 wisdom survivability summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No wisdom-preservation layer creates runtime authority.
- No prudence review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No judgment layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No wisdom framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Wisdom continuity remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, anti-technocratic, and non-centralized.
- Prudence preserves restraint under pressure without creating paralysis or elite authority.
- Strategic humility preserves dissent, uncertainty, and overconfidence records.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated wisdom-authority systems, centralized judgment-enforcement systems, or execution systems.
